labels: Bilateral pleural effusion, right greater than left. Underlying consolidation cannot be completely excluded. Recommend repositioning. NG tube terminates in stomach with sidehole in distal esophagus. Right PICC terminates in the axilla.
report:
=== VISUAL_FINDINGS ===
The frontal supine view of the chest shows:

* Normal-sized heart with normal cardiomediastinal contours
* Increased opacity in the right hemithorax, consistent with a moderate-to-large size pleural effusion
* Small pleural effusion in the left hemithorax
* Right PICC line terminating in the axilla
* Dobbhoff feeding tube and gastric tube terminating below the diaphragm
* Sidehole of the gastric tube positioned in the distal esophagus
* Extensive retrocardiac opacity in the left lower lobe, possibly indicating partial collapse of the left lower lobe
* Potential small pneumothorax on the right
* Bilateral blunting of the costophrenic angles

=== CLINICAL_EVIDENCE ===
Retrieved literature supports the presence of pleural effusions and possible pulmonary complications.

=== DEDUCTIVE_reasonING ===

Step 1: 
Increased opacity in the right hemithorax is consistent with a moderate-to-large size pleural effusion, supported by the retrieval of literature.
